pool_brief: You are given a starter index.html and a buggy script.js for a Nigerian business scenario. The script.js contains errors that prevent it from running correctly. Your task is to: (1) open the page in Chrome with DevTools open; (2) identify and fix all bugs by reading the Console error messages — state the error type and cause in a comment above each fix; (3) add console.warn() for at least one condition that should be flagged (e.g. a value below a threshold); (4) add console.error() for at least one condition that is definitely wrong (e.g. a required field is empty or a value is negative); (5) use console.table() to display any array of objects in the script. Do not guess — read the error, locate the line, understand the cause, then fix it.

project_pool.context: A Chowdeck food delivery script with three bugs: a ReferenceError (misspelled variable), a TypeError (method called on null), and missing console method improvements.

files.content:
Chowdeck Order Summary

Open DevTools (F12) → Console to see errors and output.

criteria.description: Both bugs are identified and fixed with a comment above each fix stating the error type and cause: (1) ReferenceError — 'customername' corrected to 'customerName'; (2) TypeError — subtotal.toUpperCase() removed; subtotal is a number and has no toUpperCase method. Each fix is targeted — not a random rewrite

At least one console.warn added (e.g. if subtotal < minFreeDelivery, delivery fee applies); at least one console.error added (e.g. if customerName === ''); console.table(orders) added to display the orders array; all existing console.log calls use descriptive labels

The student read the Console error message to locate each bug — demonstrated by: correct identification of the error type in the fix comment; fix targets the exact problematic identifier or method call (not a surrounding rewrite); no unrelated code was changed

camelCase naming; const used for all fixed values; no var; fix comments are clear (e.g. '// Fixed: ReferenceError — customername → customerName'); code runs to completion with correct Console output after all fixes are applied

project_pool.context: A PiggyVest savings tracker script with a SyntaxError (missing comma in object) and a ReferenceError (variable used before declaration).

files.content:
PiggyVest Savings Dashboard

Open DevTools (F12) → Console to see errors and output.

criteria.description: Both bugs are identified and fixed with comments: (1) SyntaxError — comma added after 'balance: 87500'; student notes the entire file failed to run until this was fixed; (2) ReferenceError — console.log(remaining) moved to after the const remaining declaration, or const remaining moved before the log. Each fix is explained in a comment

console.warn added if remaining > 50000 (87500 − 150000 leaves remaining = 62500, which is > 50000); console.error added if account.balance < 0; console.table added on a new transactions array (student must create this with 3 entries); existing logs have descriptive labels

Student correctly identifies the SyntaxError type and explains that the whole file failed to run; correctly identifies the ReferenceError type and explains that const/let cannot be accessed before the line they are declared; fix comments demonstrate reading the error, not random changes

camelCase naming; const for all values; no var; fix comments are precise; the created transactions array uses realistic PiggyVest data (deposits/withdrawals with dates and amounts in Naira); code runs fully after all fixes

project_pool.context: A Konga product listing script with a TypeError (method called on undefined) and a ReferenceError (misspelled object property access).

files.content:
Konga Product Listing

Open DevTools (F12) → Console to see errors and output.

criteria.description: Both bugs fixed with comments: (1) TypeError — product.rating is undefined because the product object has no 'rating' property. Fix: compute the average from the reviews array (e.g. (reviews[0].rating + reviews[1].rating + reviews[2].rating) / 3) or add a rating property to the product object; (2) ReferenceError equivalent — 'product.Category' returns undefined, not a ReferenceError (student may discover this distinction); correct fix: product.category (lowercase c). Note: property access on an object with a wrong key returns undefined rather than ReferenceError — this is an important distinction worth accepting either explanation

console.warn added if product.stock < 5 (stock is 12 so warn does not fire, but the code must be present and correct); console.error added if product.price <= 0 (price is 89000 so error does not fire, but must be present and correct); console.table(reviews) added; all existing logs have descriptive labels

Student correctly identifies the TypeError and explains that calling .toFixed(1) on undefined fails; explains the undefined property access issue for product.Category; fix comments demonstrate understanding rather than random changes
camelCase throughout; const used correctly; no var; fix comments are clear and accurate; final script runs to completion with no errors; console.table displays a readable grid of reviews

project_pool.context: A Paystack transaction summary script with a SyntaxError (missing closing bracket) and a TypeError (calling a function on a non-function value).

files.content:
Paystack Transaction Report

Open DevTools (F12) → Console to see errors and output.

criteria.description: Both bugs fixed with comments: (1) SyntaxError — missing closing parenthesis on console.log('Merchant:', merchant; — add the closing ) so it becomes console.log('Merchant:', merchant); Student must note the file did not run at all until this was fixed; (2) TypeError — feeRate() treats feeRate as a function; feeRate is a number (0.014). Fix: remove the () so it reads console.log('Fee rate:', feeRate);

console.warn added if processingFee > 2000 (processingFee = 125000 * 0.014 = 1750, so warn does NOT fire — but the condition must be correctly written); console.error added if totalAmount <= 0 (125000 > 0 so error does not fire — but must be present); console.table(transactions) added; all existing logs have descriptive labels

Student identifies SyntaxError type and notes the whole file failed to run; identifies TypeError type and explains that feeRate is a number not a function; fix comments are targeted at the specific problem
camelCase naming; const used correctly; no var; fix comments accurate; all logs have labels; arithmetic (processingFee = 1750, netAmount = 123250) is correct in the output after all fixes applied

project_pool.context: A Bolt ride analytics script with a ReferenceError (variable out of scope) and a TypeError (string method on number), plus missing console improvements.

files.content:
Bolt Ride Analytics

Open DevTools (F12) → Console to see errors and output.

criteria.description: Both bugs fixed with comments: (1) TypeError — totalTrips.includes('8') fails because totalTrips is a number and numbers have no includes() method. Fix: convert to string first — String(totalTrips).includes('8') → returns true (843 contains '8'); or use a different check such as totalTrips % 100 === 43; (2) ReferenceError — 'avgrating' should be 'avgRating' (capital R). JavaScript is case-sensitive. Fix: console.log('Average rating:', avgRating);

console.warn added if avgRating < 4.5 (avgRating is 4.92 so warn does not fire — but condition must be correctly written); console.error added if totalTrips === 0 (843 !== 0 so error does not fire — but must be present); console.table(recentRides) added; all existing logs have descriptive labels

Student correctly identifies the TypeError and explains includes() is a string/array method not available on numbers; correctly identifies the ReferenceError and the case mismatch; fix comments demonstrate reading and understanding the error messages
camelCase throughout; const used correctly; no var; fix comments are clear; recentRides table displays correctly after fix; script runs to completion with no errors
